1.description: Risk-taking in the example. In Panels A and B, equilibrium performance distributions under bonus, rank, and mixed rewards are plotted forS(Panel A) andW(Panel B). Panel C provides summary statistics. Equilibrium performance distributions: with probability 0.20,XW=0$X_W = 0$; with probability 0.20,XW∼Uniform[7,8]$X_W \sim \text{Uniform}[7, 8]$; with probability 0.40,XW∼Uniform[0,2]$X_W \sim \text{Uniform}[0, 2]$; with probability 0.25,XS=7=θ$X_S = 7 = \theta$; with probability 0.25,XS∼Uniform[7,8]$X_S \sim \text{Uniform}[7, 8]$; and with probability 0.50,XS∼Uniform[0,2]$X_S \sim \text{Uniform}[0, 2]$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Equilibrium rewards in Eq2 equilibria, where bothSandWchase the bonus. The figure illustrates the reward functionsΠi$\Pi _i$and support linesαi+βix$\alpha _i + \beta _i\,x$for manageri=S$i = S$(Panel A) and manageri=W$i = W$(Panel B). The horizontal axis represents performance,x. The parameters areμS=3$\mu _S=3$,μW=1$\mu _W=1$,θ=6$\theta =6$, andr=2/3$r={2}/{3}$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Equilibrium rewards in Eq1 equilibria, where onlySchases the bonus. The figure illustrates the reward functionsΠi$\Pi _i$and the support linesαi+βix$\alpha _i + \beta _i\,x$for manageri=S$i = S$(Panel A) and manageri=W$i =W$(Panel B). The horizontal axis represents performance,x. The parameters areμS=3$\mu _S=3$,μW=1$\mu _W=1$,θ=7$\theta =7$, andr=5$r=5$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: Parametric curves for equilibrium configurations. In the figure, the blue line represents the Eq1/Eq2 boundary and the red line represents the Eq0/Eq1 boundary. In Panel A,(μS,μW)=(2,1)$(\mu _S,\mu _W)=(2,1)$. In Panel B,(μS,μW)=(7,1)$(\mu _S,\mu _W)=(7,1)$. The dotted vertical line represents points whereθ=2μS$\theta =2\,\mu _S$, maximum performance under rank competition. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Bonus targeting bySand RRT byW. The figure plots a parametric curve where the parameter is managers' rank focus,r. The abscissa of each point on the curve represents the probability the strong manager targets the bonus,pSθ$p_S^{\theta }$, and the ordinate represents the ruin risk accepted by the weak manager,pW0$p_W^0$. The dashed line divides the plane between the points on the curve under which the level of rank focus supports an Eq1 or Eq2 configuration. The point at the left end of the curve represents bonus targeting and RRT in an Eq0 configuration. The fixed parameters in the figure areμS=4$\mu _S= 4$,μW=1$\mu _W = 1$, andθ=9$\theta = 9$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: Upside risk-taking. In this example,μS=2$\mu _S=2$,μW=1$\mu _W=1$, andθ=3$\theta =3$.2μS$2\, \mu _S$is the maximum performance level under rank competition, while θ represents the maximum performance level under bonus competition. The function graphed represents maximum performance in mixed competitions as a function of the rank focus parameter,r.

7.description: Stochastic dominance and parameter regions. In the figure, the gray shaded region labeled “Eq2-TSD” represents the region in which the equilibrium configuration under mixed rewards is Eq2 and the performance distributions of both the strong and weak managers third-order stochastically dominate their performance distributions under bonus rewards. The region labeled “Eq1” represents the region in which the equilibrium configuration under mixed rewards is Eq1, and the region labeled “Eq2-noSD” represents the region in which the equilibrium configuration under mixed rewards is Eq2 but performance distributions are not ordered by first-, second-, or third-order stochastic dominance. In the figure, the rank and bonus rewards are equal when rewards are mixed (i.e.,r=1$r = 1$).
